description: Stonewright prompt library: outcome-oriented starters for MCP clients. Prefer stonewright-task-start; do not paste full prompts into task-start responses.

prompts.title: Inspect site snapshot
prompts.summary: Capture a compact operational overview before changing anything.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright on this WordPress site. Call stonewright-task-start first. Then run stonewright-site-snapshot and stonewright-content-inventory. Summarize name, theme, plugin counts, post counts, mode, MCP surface, and the top content types. Do not mutate the site.
prompts.verification: Response includes mode, mcp_surface, and at least post/page counts without write tools.

prompts.title: Repair from site pulse
prompts.summary: Score hardening/performance and apply the top safe fixes.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Start with stonewright-task-start, then stonewright-site-pulse. For each failed high/critical check, propose a concrete repair. Apply only reversible option/config fixes after confirming mode; skip production-safe destructive steps without tokens.
prompts.verification: Pulse score and fixes list present; any writes audited; no confirmation_token skipped in production-safe.

prompts.title: Elementor landing page from brief
prompts.summary: Build a new Elementor landing page with native widgets first.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start. Plan with stonewright-design-native-plan, validate the DesignSpec, snapshot, then build with stonewright-elementor-v3-build-page-from-spec. Polish with stonewright-elementor-v3-batch-mutate. Prefer native Elementor widgets. Verify desktop/tablet/mobile with no horizontal overflow.
prompts.verification: Page has Elementor data; digest shows structure; no raw HTML widget dump for core sections.

prompts.title: Elementor section refactor
prompts.summary: Refactor one hero/section with backup and batch mutate.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright on page {{post_id}}. Start with stonewright-task-start, read stonewright-elementor-page-digest, snapshot via backup, then batch-mutate only the hero section for clearer hierarchy and spacing. Leave other sections untouched.
prompts.verification: Backup snapshot id recorded; only targeted elements changed; digest before/after differs in hero only.

prompts.title: Gutenberg homepage hero
prompts.summary: Update a block-theme homepage hero without breaking brand tokens.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start. Read the homepage template and current hero blocks. Snapshot before writes. Update the hero for stronger headline, CTA, and mobile spacing while preserving theme.json tokens. Verify desktop and mobile.
prompts.verification: Template/post updated; theme.json tokens unchanged unless explicitly requested.

prompts.title: FSE footer cleanup
prompts.summary: Clean footer template part structure.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Start with stonewright-task-start. Read the footer template part, snapshot, then simplify navigation/social rows for accessibility and consistent spacing. Do not invent brand colors outside theme.json.
prompts.verification: Footer part saves; no horizontal overflow at 390px width.

prompts.title: Apply a brand kit
prompts.summary: List and apply a built-in brand kit carefully.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start, list brand kits, pick the kit that best matches the site brief, and apply it. Report which tokens changed and any follow-up Elementor kit steps.
prompts.verification: Kit id applied; token diff summarized; destructive paths token-gated in production-safe.

prompts.title: Apply industry blueprint
prompts.summary: Scaffold pages from a Stonewright blueprint.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Start with stonewright-task-start. List blueprints, choose the best industry match for this site, get the blueprint details, then apply with a clear page map. Prefer dry-run or review mode when available; confirm titles before publish.
prompts.verification: Blueprint apply result lists created/updated pages; no silent overwrites without snapshot.

prompts.title: Services CPT + fields
prompts.summary: Register a Services model and seed sample rows.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start. Create a Services content model with fields for icon, headline, summary, proof metric, and CTA. Seed six service rows with bulk upsert. Finish with content-inventory to confirm counts.
prompts.verification: CPT visible in inventory; six seeded posts; REST base documented.

prompts.title: Stock image fill for hero
prompts.summary: Search Openverse/stock, import, attach alt text.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Start with stonewright-task-start. Search stock images for the homepage hero brief, import the best licensed result, set meaningful alt text, and report the attachment id for the page builder to use.
prompts.verification: Attachment id returned; alt non-empty; license/source noted when available.

prompts.title: On-page SEO meta pass
prompts.summary: Inspect and update SEO meta for key pages.
prerequisites: SEO plugin with REST or Stonewright SEO adapters
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start and stonewright-seo-status. For the homepage and top three service pages, get meta, tighten titles/descriptions to intent, update, and report before/after strings.
prompts.verification: Meta updated only on listed ids; titles ≤ ~60 chars; no keyword stuffing.

prompts.title: WP-CLI cache and rewrite health
prompts.summary: Tokenized WP-CLI status, rewrite flush when safe.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright companion WP-CLI tools only (never shell wp). Call stonewright-task-start, wp-cli-status, discover rewrite/cache commands, then run a safe status check. Flush rewrites only if the operator confirmed staging.
prompts.verification: All WP-CLI calls tokenized; no eval/shell; output includes command results without secrets.

prompts.title: Apply blueprint on Elementor engine
prompts.summary: Explicit Elementor engine for a blueprint apply.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start first. List blueprints, pick the best industry match, then call stonewright-blueprint-apply with engine=elementor (never silently fall back). Prefer draft status. Snapshot before write; report created page ids and any unsupported nodes.
prompts.verification: Apply uses engine=elementor; draft pages created; unsupported_nodes empty or listed honestly.

prompts.title: Apply blueprint on Gutenberg engine
prompts.summary: Explicit Gutenberg engine for a blueprint apply.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start first. Apply the chosen blueprint with engine=gutenberg. Validate blocks with parse/apply helpers. Prefer draft. Do not invent competitor product names.
prompts.verification: engine=gutenberg; blocks parse cleanly; draft saved.

prompts.title: Apply blueprint toward FSE templates
prompts.summary: FSE-oriented blueprint path with snapshot.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start. Snapshot FSE state, then apply the blueprint with engine=fse when available (or report stonewright_engine_unavailable honestly). Never bypass backup/snapshot gates.
prompts.verification: Snapshot taken; FSE engine success or structured unavailable error; no silent fallback.

prompts.title: Brand kit preview and restore point
prompts.summary: Preview brand kit impact and keep a restore handle.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start. List kits, then brand-kit-apply with preview=true when supported to get a before/after diff. Apply for real only after operator approval and capture restore_id.
prompts.verification: Diff or change report present; restore_id when applied; production-safe tokens respected.

prompts.title: Figma to native pixel-perfect build
prompts.summary: External Figma MCP to evidence to native plan to verify.
prerequisites: External Figma MCP tools in the client (official and/or console-bridge)
design-to-wordpress skill references/figma-mcp-extraction.md for field-to-tool map
Browser/Playwright MCP for post-write measured_targets verification
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start. List MCP tools and extract design with the client's external Figma MCP (console-bridge preferred when present: figma_export_tokens / figma_get_variables / deep component trees; official: get_variable_defs, get_design_context, get_metadata, get_screenshot). Map fields via skills/design-to-wordpress/references/figma-mcp-extraction.md. Normalize into DesignEvidence/figma_token_table (never embed Figma APIs inside Stonewright; no raw Figma tree after normalization). Call stonewright-design-native-plan. Render with the user-chosen engine (elementor|gutenberg|fse). CSS only when native_gap is recorded. Verify each breakpoint with the client's own browser tools against evidence targets (±2px spacing).
prompts.verification: Native plan present; native_gap for any CSS; no raw Figma tree after normalization; provenance set on measured values; verification notes per breakpoint.

prompts.title: Upgrade tool profile mid-session
prompts.summary: When a needed tool is missing, activate a broader profile and re-list.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start. If a required tool is missing from the list, call stonewright-tool-profile with action=activate and profile=full (or the matching specialist profile). Then re-list tools (tools/list) before continuing. Do not invent shell or REST workarounds.
prompts.verification: tool-profile returns tools_changed/re_list_instruction when applicable; subsequent tools/list includes the needed tool.

prompts.title: WooCommerce catalog bootstrap
prompts.summary: Products, categories, and stock-safe content ops.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start. Inventory products/categories. Seed a small catalog (≤12 products) with bulk upsert, attach images, and keep drafts until review. No payment gateway or live checkout changes without explicit operator approval.
prompts.verification: Inventory counts increase; products remain draft unless approved; no payment settings mutated.

prompts.title: Elementor transactional repair
prompts.summary: Batch mutate under transaction envelope with rollback.
prompts.prompt: Use Stonewright. Call stonewright-task-start. Digest the page, then stonewright-elementor-v3-transaction-run with stop_on_error and rollback_on_error. Prefer native widgets. If readback fails, confirm automatic rollback and report snapshot id.
prompts.verification: Transaction result ok or rolled_back with snapshot; no partial silent failure.
